mission.title: Install and Configure Konveyor on Kubernetes
mission.description: Konveyor is an open-source project that provides tools for developers and platform teams to analyze and transform legacy applications for Kubernetes and cloud-native technologies. Installing the Konveyor Operator allows you to manage the deployment and lifecycle of Konveyor on your Kubernetes cluster.

steps.title: Add the Konveyor Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Konveyor Helm repository to your local Helm configuration to access the Konveyor charts.
```bash
helm repo add konveyor https://move2kube.konveyor.io
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install the Konveyor Operator
steps.description:
Install the Konveyor Operator using Helm. This command will create a new namespace called `konveyor` and deploy the operator within it.
```bash
helm install konveyor konveyor/konveyor --namespace konveyor --create-namespace --version v99.0.0
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Konveyor Operator pod is running successfully in the `konveyor` namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace konveyor
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Configure resource limits for the Konveyor Operator to ensure it has adequate resources. Edit the Helm release to set resource limits.
```bash
helm upgrade konveyor konveyor/konveyor --namespace konveyor --set resources.limits.cpu=500m --set resources.limits.memory=512Mi
```

steps.title: Set up Ingress (optional)
steps.description:
If you want to expose the Konveyor UI, set up Ingress by creating an Ingress resource. Replace `<your-domain>` with your actual domain.
```yaml
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: konveyor-ingress
  namespace: konveyor
spec:
  rules:
  - host: <your-domain>
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: konveyor-ui
            port:
              number: 80
```
Apply the Ingress resource:
```bash
kubectl apply -f konveyor-ingress.yaml
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will show the Konveyor Operator pod running in the `konveyor` namespace. You can access the Konveyor UI through the configured Ingress if set up.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add konveyor https://move2kube.konveyor.io
helm repo update
helm install konveyor konveyor/konveyor --namespace konveyor --create-namespace --version v99.0.0
helm upgrade konveyor konveyor/konveyor --namespace konveyor --set resources.limits.cpu=500m --set resources.limits.memory=512Mi

apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: konveyor-ingress
  namespace: konveyor
spec:
  rules:
  - host: <your-domain>
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: konveyor-ui
            port:
              number: 80

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Konveyor Operator release from your Kubernetes cluster using Helm. This will remove the deployment and associated resources from the `konveyor` namespace.
```bash
helm uninstall konveyor --namespace konveyor
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent volume claims (PVCs) that were created during the installation. This ensures that all resources are cleaned up.
```bash
kubectl delete crd $(kubectl get crd | grep konveyor | awk '{print $1}')
kubectl delete pvc --all --namespace konveyor
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Delete the `konveyor` namespace to remove any remaining resources and verify that it has been successfully deleted.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace konveyor
kubectl get namespaces
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current state of your Konveyor installation. This can be done by exporting the current configuration and resources.
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace konveyor -o yaml > konveyor-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository and upgrade the Konveyor Operator to the latest version. Ensure you specify the correct version.
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade konveyor konveyor/konveyor --namespace konveyor --version v99.1.0
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the Konveyor Operator pod is running successfully after the upgrade. Ensure that the version is updated.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace konveyor
kubectl describe deployment konveyor --namespace konveyor | grep Image
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If the Konveyor Operator pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors. This may indicate a misconfiguration or resource issue.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace konveyor
```

troubleshooting.title: Ingress not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If the Ingress resource is not reachable, verify the Ingress configuration and check the service endpoints. Ensure that the Ingress controller is running.
```bash
kubectl describe ingress konveyor-ingress --namespace konveyor
kubectl get svc --namespace konveyor
```

troubleshooting.title: Resource limits causing OOMKilled
troubleshooting.description:
If pods are being killed due to out-of-memory (OOM) errors, check the resource limits set for the Konveyor Operator and adjust them accordingly.
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace konveyor | grep -i oom
```

troubleshooting.title: Helm upgrade fails with version conflict
troubleshooting.description:
If the Helm upgrade fails due to a version conflict, ensure that the specified version is available in the Helm repository and that there are no pending changes.
```bash
helm search repo konveyor/konveyor --versions
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with OLM and Ingress support.
